life_history: Elena has a complex family dynamic characterized by recurrent conflicts and strained relationships. She faces issues with trust and boundaries among family members, some of whom struggle with alcoholism. Elena feels that a relative has taken advantage of her generosity, leading to a general frustration with those around her. She has a history of significant aggressive behavior and violence, which further complicates her interpersonal relationships.
core_belief_description: Helpless: I am helpless. I am powerless, weak, vulnerable.
Unlovable: I am unlovable. I am bound to be alone.
intermediate_beliefs: People always take advantage of me.
I need to be assertive to not be a victim.
I must protect myself from being hurt by others.
intermediate_beliefs_during_depression: When I'm depressed, I believe that I will always be alone because nobody cares, I'm unlovable, and it's useless to try because people will disappoint me.
coping_strategies: Avoiding social interactions to prevent feeling overwhelmed by rage.
Isolating at home, often staying in the bedroom.

cognitive_models.situation: Reflecting on the prospect of spending time alone during holidays.
cognitive_models.automatic_thoughts: I am always the one giving and never receiving. People only remember me when they need something.
cognitive_models.behavior: Expresses resentment and continues to ruminate on past unreciprocated generosity, reinforcing isolation.

cognitive_models.situation: Considering reaching out to an estranged individual who has hurt me.
cognitive_models.automatic_thoughts: He may come back and say sorry, but it won't be genuine. It will be the same cycle all over again.
cognitive_models.behavior: Debates internally whether to re-initiate contact, ultimately choosing not to out of self-protection.
